current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.
architecture_findings: round 1: A gated SAE with a separate support-selection encoder can reduce support interference versus plain TopK at K=128, improving FVU before spending effort on smaller-K or cost optimizations. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=identical_to_baseline)

round 2: Run the newly added gated SAE prototype at the existing K=128 anchor to get the first quality signal on whether separate support gating can materially improve FVU over plain TopK. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=, k=None, health=normal, termination=completed)

failure_patterns.pattern: A gated SAE with a separate support-selection encoder can reduce support interference versus plain TopK at K=128, improving FVU before spending effort on smaller-K or cost optimizations.
Run the newly added gated SAE prototype at the existing K=128 anchor to get the first quality signal on whether separate support gating can materially improve FVU over plain TopK.

gated.design_hypothesis: Run the newly added gated SAE prototype at the existing K=128 anchor to get the first quality signal on whether separate support gating can materially improve FVU over plain TopK.
next_steps: If gated improves proxy FVU, do a gated-family stabilization pass on gate sharpness or magnitude defaults.
If gated is quality-neutral but slower, separate implementation cost from architectural value before discarding it.
If gated clearly regresses FVU, pivot to a different architecture family rather than spending many rounds on local tuning.
